raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The bill would ban the sale of products containing toxic chemicals in California.",
    "AB 495 will protect consumers by banning the sale in California of cosmetics containing known carcinogens, reproductive toxins, and endocrine disruptors that are harmful to human health.",
    "The full list of the 'Toxic Twenty' chemicals that would be banned from use under the Toxic-Free Cosmetics Act includes asbestos, lead, diethylhexyl phthalate, formaldehyde, mercury and related compounds, and others.",
    "Lead is classified as a neurotoxin.",
    "Formaldehyde is classified as a carcinogen.",
    "Diethylhexyl phthalate is classified as a reproductive and developmental toxicant.",
    "Mercury and related compounds are classified as neurotoxins.",
    "Parabens are classified as reproductive and developmental toxicants and hormone disruptors.",
    "Toluene is classified as a reproductive and developmental toxicant.",
    "Triclosan is classified as a hormone disruptor.",
    "Carbon black is classified as a carcinogen.",
    "Fluorinated PFAS compounds are classified as carcinogens and developmental toxicants.",
    "The California Safe Cosmetics Program requires major cosmetics manufacturers to provide notification whenever they sell a cosmetics product that contains a reproductive toxicant or a carcinogen."
  ]
}
```
